lectionary.gospelTitle: The Parable of the Fig Tree

readings.summary: The Lord calls Moses from a burning bush.

readings.citation:
Ps 103: 1-2, 3-4, 6-7, 8, 11, R. v.8a
The Lord is kind and merciful.
readings.antiphon:
Ps 103: 1-2, 3-4, 6-7, 8, 11, R. v.8a
The Lord is kind and merciful.

readings.summary: We must learn from the example of our ancestors.

Repent, says the Lord;
the kingdom of heaven is at hand.
The parable of the fig tree.

antiphons.text:
My eyes are always on the Lord,
for he rescues my feet from the snare.
Turn to me and have mercy on me,
for I am alone and poor.

antiphons.citation: Entrance Antiphon • Option II | Cf. Ez 36:23–26
antiphons.text:
When I prove my holiness among you,
I will gather you from all the foreign lands;
and I will pour clean water upon you
and cleanse you from all your impurities,
and I will give you a new spirit, says the Lord.
The sparrow finds a home, and the swallow a nest for her young: by your altars, O Lord of hosts, my King and my God. Blessed are they who dwell in your house, for ever singing your praise.
